_comment: Water estimates in milliliters per token. Two scopes: conservative (company-reported, on-site cooling only) and academic (full lifecycle including electricity generation water). Update as new research emerges.
_sources: Li et al. 2023 - Making AI Less Thirsty (UC Riverside / UT Arlington) - arxiv.org/abs/2304.03271
Google official disclosure - Measuring the environmental impact of AI inference - cloud.google.com/blog - Aug 2025
Sam Altman public statement - ChatGPT uses 0.32ml per query - June 2025
Jegham et al. May 2025 - How Hungry is AI? arxiv.org/abs/2505.09598
de Vries-Gao 2025 - AI water footprint in Patterns journal - Dec 2025
IEEE Spectrum - The Real Story on AI Water Usage at Data Centers - Sep 2025
moduledge.com AI Water Usage Per-Query Cost analysis - Apr 2026
_methodology: Conservative scope = on-site cooling water only (as reported by companies). Anchor: Google disclosed 0.26ml per median Gemini text prompt (scope 1+2 on-site, Aug 2025); Sam Altman confirmed 0.32ml per ChatGPT query (scope 1, June 2025). Academic scope = full lifecycle including water consumed for electricity generation. Anchor: UC Riverside/IEEE estimates 16.9ml per 150-300 word output in average US data center. Per-token values derived from per-query estimates divided by ~500 tokens (median query size). Model tiers scaled by compute intensity: large=100%, medium=65%, small=40%, tiny=25%.

_notes.gemini_official: Google officially disclosed 0.26ml per median Gemini text prompt (Aug 2025, scope 1+2 on-site). Used as anchor for conservative large-tier estimate.
_notes.chatgpt_official: Sam Altman confirmed 0.32ml per ChatGPT query (June 2025, scope 1 only). Consistent with Google anchor.
_notes.claude_official: Anthropic has not disclosed per-query water figures as of May 2026. Estimates use comparable frontier model data.
_notes.academic_range: UC Riverside/IEEE research estimates 16.9ml per 150-300 word output in average US data center (full lifecycle). Li et al. 2023 found ~500ml per 20-50 queries = ~10-25ml per query (scope 1+2).
_notes.uncertainty: Water usage varies 10-25x based on datacenter location, cooling method, season, and local climate. Liquid-cooled (closed-loop) facilities reduce direct water use by 70-90%. These are order-of-magnitude estimates only.
_notes.efficiency_trend: Models are 10-33x more water-efficient per output token than 2023 estimates due to hardware and software improvements, but total data center consumption continues rising with scale.
